title: aureolaria patula
intro: Aureolaria patula, also known as the greenish-yellow sticky ladyslipper or sticky ladyslipper orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. This parasitic plant, native to the mountainous regions of North America, obtains its nutrients by tapping into the vascular system of surrounding plants. The medicinal properties of Aureolaria patula lie in its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory and antiseptic agent, which can be attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Herbal preparations of Aureolaria patula can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves, and are believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, soothing skin irritations, and promoting wound healing.
